3. How to Deal With Inbound Leads Who Only Filled Out a Form.mp4 Okay, so when we move away from the model of only dealing with leads who had booked a call, only dealing with leads who had already watched some type of sales-based video, and we move to the updated model, which is trending towards what consumers are currently doing. Filling out a form. They're fully willing to talk with you. They totally understand what the offer is. They understand the details of the offer. They filled out several questions and you've got that data right in front of you. What do you do as soon as you get that lead? First things first, we have to address current consumer trends, okay? You yourself, right now, as a salesperson, you're likely one of the few people left in the entire market of people who have cell phones that... does not have do not disturb constantly on, and that allows any random number to call through to your phone. Most regular people in the world, just the general public as a whole, potential buyers. What they've had happen to them over these past several years, they've gotten the absolute shit spammed out of them. They've had political numbers call them. They've had all these random scammers call them. They've had tons of people who shouldn't have their phone number just hammering their phone over the last several years in a row. They've gotten texts like that. It's gotten pretty crazy. So, iPhone, as an example, has that little feature inside of your settings now that just simply says 'silenced unknown callers.' And it just means if some random number calls you. Your phone is automatically going to silence that call. It's not even going to ring through. The other instance is people like me right now is an example. If I filled out a form for your offer, I'm in the middle of filming right now. My phone's on 'do not disturb.' If you just called my phone, there's probably six or seven other people that have also called my phone that I don't have saved in my contact list, by the way. There's not a chance. There's not a chance after I end this very session— I'm just gonna pick up my phone and go through my missed calls and dial each random number that's called me. Nobody does that. It's an illusion if you think so. So what we want to do— that doesn't mean we want to give up on calling people. That means we also want to make sure that we're texting them. So as soon as an inbound lead comes in where I've got somebody who filled out a form and has expressed interest in working with the business, I'm immediately gonna contact that person. Yeah, I'm gonna shoot him a call, but here's a little trick. On iPhone as an example, if I send the person a message that says, 'Hey, it's Jeremy.' Then I call them. The phone will say the random number, but it'll also say 'maybe Jeremy' below it. So a little trick to potentially get people to answer is just by shooting a text message to them first. In addition to that, if somebody does not answer the phone or once again, especially if it rings through the voicemail the first time, I'm immediately gonna call back because if 'Do not disturb' is turned on, if somebody calls me once, it'll go to voicemail. If somebody calls me twice, it will ring through. And as I mentioned, if you so happen to have somebody that rings through after that second call and it says 'Maybe Jeremy' as an example. I'm much more probable to answer that call than I am if you dial once and then simply never call me back again. Now, even if we get that second dial to go through and they don't answer, or even if they do answer, we wanna make sure that we text people. We wanna make sure that we are biasing towards text-first communication. So the big thing, once again, when a lead comes in, we want to text. with our name. Okay. The name is the most important, once again, because that tricks iPhone. to potentially letting your call go through. We also want to make sure we text in what I refer to as a very open-ended communication. So we want to text like an open-ended question. We want to acknowledge the behavior. and then we want to ask an open-ended question. Let me give you an example of this. So I'm going to say something like, 'Hey Leo, it's Jeremy. I saw you just filled out a form of ours online. Would love to chat with you. If you are open in the next five minutes, if not, I have some time later today. Let me know if that works, or shoot over whatever questions you have Now, I've given them a few options in that example, and I want to tell you something very important, and it's so important you understand this. It's the ratio of reducing my nose, okay? So as an example, if I just say, 'Hey, do you have five minutes?' That's a 50-50. What do I mean? Well, it's 50% yes, 50% no. Do you want to talk now? That's just a 50-50, yes or no. That means I have a 50% chance of a no happening versus remember what I just said. Hey Leo, it's Jeremy. I saw you just filled out a form of ours online. If you're open in the next five minutes, I can give you a call now or I can talk later. Maybe you just have some questions for me. You can also text me here. Three options there. So what did I do? I reduced my no to a 25% chance and I reduced positive communication to a 75% chance. You see that? So I want to reduce the no's as low as I can. In comparison to up here, I have a 50% odds of no. Hey, can you jump on the phone right now? 50% no. Hey, I got time in the next five minutes if you can talk to me. or I can do later today. Or you can just shoot me over some questions right here via text. Let me know. 75% chance odds of a yes. Remember, if people are not really probable to answer the phone anyway, I want to make sure I bias towards text. Okay, I do want to be very clear. I do still want to call at least twice. Because if I get voicemail first, especially, there's a high probability that person's just on do not disturb mode. So I want to make sure I call twice. So remember, once I get a lead that is filled out of form, it's also very important that I reach out to that person fast. Now, listen, I'm a marketer, guys, okay? I'm not a sales trainer. I'm doing all this to give you some additional perspective. I want to make this very clear. Thank you. With the strategies that we run for people. They get very overwhelmed with the quantity of leads that they have very quickly. And I respect your day-to-day. I want you to know that. I know that if you wake up, you might already have, like, four to eight scheduled calls in your calendar that day. You and I both know there's gonna be some people that don't show up. And in that free time, when new leads come in, we all— the business owner, myself, hopefully yourself, and your people in your sales team as well—will expect you to use that time to reach out to people as fast as you can, okay? If you're currently working in a very small team, maybe you're one of the only people that's... currently taking all the inbound leads. Look, we all get it. You're going to get a little overwhelmed and there's going to be people who don't get communicated to right away. But I want you to always remember, there's so many statistics out there that validate the fact that when you text people quickly or when you call them quickly— typically within five minutes. Listen to this statistic. If you text or call quickly, there is a 50% chance that they close. Or that they're at least willing to communicate to you. And no, that's not a made-up statistic. It's validated by a fuckload of data. So to be clear, I just want to reiterate: I totally understand if you are not... Not capable of reaching out to somebody as fast as within five minutes, as an example, but you wanna make sure that anytime you have the availability to do so, that you do so, because there's a much higher chance that these people are actually going to communicate with you when you do so. So once again, when a lead comes in, simply put: I want to call at least twice. I want to text with my name to trick the iPhone to show up as maybe X. I also want to make sure that I text the open-ended question. I want to make sure that I'm going to reduce my odds of a no to as little as I can. I don't want to overwhelm people. I want to make sure I say, 'Hey, I got five minutes open now.' I'm open in the next five minutes. I got time later today if you can't make it right now, or you can text me some questions if you have any. Boom, I reduced my chances of 'no' to 25%. That's what you want to focus on as soon as a lead comes in. Let's move to the next video to see what we should do next.
